insights:
The single best deal on the market today is MiMo-V2.5. It achieves the highest composite value score of 816.7, driven by a strong intelligence rating of 49 out of 60 and a blended price of just $0.06 per million tokens. With a speed of 81 tokens per second, a 1000K context window, and arena ELO scores of 1433 (general) and 1435 (code), it delivers top-tier capability at a fraction of the cost of comparable models. No other model offers this combination of high intelligence and low price.

Two notable runners-up deserve consideration. DeepSeek V4 Flash (standard variant) scores 47/60 intelligence at the same $0.06 price, with a faster 94 t/s throughput and identical 1000K context, making it a close second with a value score of 783.3. For applications requiring even higher throughput, MiMo-V2-Flash (Feb 2026) pushes speed to 151 t/s while maintaining a 256K context and a 41/60 intelligence score, all at the same $0.06 rate. Its value score of 683.3 is lower but acceptable for latency-sensitive tasks. Alternatively, StepFun Step 3.5 Flash offers 196 t/s speed and 38/60 intelligence for the same price, though its smaller 256K context limits some use cases.

For cost-sensitive API calls, the clear recommendation is MiMo-V2.5. It provides the highest intelligence-to-price ratio, suitable for most production workloads including reasoning, coding, and general chat. If your budget is extremely tight and you can tolerate very low intelligence (10/60), Nova Micro at $0.03 per million tokens and 296 t/s speed is the cheapest option, but its limited context (130K) and poor benchmark scores make it suitable only for trivial tasks. For the vast majority of cost-conscious developers, MiMo-V2.5 strikes the optimal balance.
